mapper.select: SELECT id AS user_id, unit_id, parent_user_id, level_no, tree_path FROM sys_user WHERE id = #{userId} AND is_deleted = FALSE

SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.parent_user_id, u.unit_id, u.level_no, u.tree_path, u.username, u.real_name, u.job_title, u.mobile, u.status FROM sys_user u WHERE u.unit_id = #{unitId} AND u.is_deleted = FALSE AND u.tree_path LIKE #{treePathPrefix} ORDER BY u.level_no, u.id

SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.parent_user_id, u.unit_id, u.level_no, u.tree_path, u.username, u.real_name, u.job_title, u.mobile, u.status FROM sys_user u WHERE u.unit_id = #{unitId} AND u.parent_user_id = #{parentUserId} AND u.is_deleted = FALSE ORDER BY u.level_no, u.id

SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.parent_user_id, u.unit_id, u.level_no, u.tree_path, u.username, u.real_name, u.job_title, u.mobile, u.status FROM sys_user u WHERE u.unit_id = #{unitId} AND u.is_deleted = FALSE AND #{treePath} LIKE u.tree_path || '%' ORDER BY u.level_no, u.id
mapper.update: UPDATE sys_user SET parent_user_id = #{parentUserId}, unit_id = COALESCE(#{unitId}, unit_id), level_no = #{levelNo}, tree_path = #{treePath} WHERE id = #{userId} AND is_deleted = FALSE

UPDATE sys_user SET parent_user_id = CASE WHEN id = #{userId} THEN #{newParentUserId} ELSE parent_user_id END, level_no = level_no + #{levelOffset}, tree_path = #{newTreePath} || substring(tree_path from char_length(#{oldTreePath}) + 1) WHERE tree_path = #{oldTreePath} OR tree_path LIKE #{oldTreePath} || '%'
